title: Proof Card 001 - Buyer Rescue Workflow
coreStory: A buyer needs support. GoalOS improves the customer-support workflow. AGIALPHA coordinates sponsor, builder, and reviewer proof work. Evidence is validated. A Proof Card, credential, and reputation update make the improvement reusable.

contractFlow.plainEnglish: Sponsor records mission aim, success criteria, constraints, authority, data boundary, rollback obligation, and claim boundary.
contractFlow.whyItMatters: The mission is governed before work starts.

contractFlow.plainEnglish: Operator records plan graph, tool permissions, context root, policy root, budget, and latency limits.
contractFlow.whyItMatters: The route is auditable before execution.

contractFlow.plainEnglish: Sponsor posts the support-workflow proof mission and escrows an AGIALPHA reward.
contractFlow.whyItMatters: A useful problem becomes a public proof mission.

contractFlow.plainEnglish: Builder claims the mission with an AGIALPHA claim bond.
contractFlow.whyItMatters: The builder has commitment and a deadline.

contractFlow.plainEnglish: Builder submits proof hash, proof URI, proof-card hash, and proof bond.
contractFlow.whyItMatters: Private work stays off-chain; public proof anchor is created.

contractFlow.plainEnglish: Reviewer bonds AGIALPHA before validating evidence.
Reviewer validates the before/after workflow, scorecard, claim boundary, and proof note.
contractFlow.whyItMatters: The proof passes or is rejected with reasons.

contractFlow.plainEnglish: Public-safe Proof Card is registered for the approved workflow improvement.
contractFlow.whyItMatters: The public can understand what improved without seeing private data.

contractFlow.plainEnglish: Builder receives a non-transferable proof-work credential.
contractFlow.whyItMatters: Approved work becomes a portable trust signal.

contractFlow.plainEnglish: Builder and reviewer reputation update after approved proof.
contractFlow.whyItMatters: Future missions can route by verified reputation.

contractFlow.plainEnglish: Evidence Docket anchors claims, costs, risks, public/private boundary, and proof artifacts.
If the improvement passes the gate, it can influence future work and is recorded in institutional memory.
contractFlow.whyItMatters: This is RSI: only verified improvements earn propagation rights.

rsiLoop.meaning: ProofBundle and Evidence Docket show what changed.
Reviewer and Selection Gate approve only if evidence and risk controls pass.

claimBoundary: Illustrative public usage example. Not a live customer result, not financial/legal/tax/security advice, and not Ethereum Mainnet deployment authorization.
